scenarios.name: Scalability First
scenarios.description: Scalability promoted to the highest-priority criterion, followed by Expressiveness, Extensibility, Accessibility, Maturity

rankings.notes: Tied at Adequate Scalability with PowerModels/PowerSimulations/GridCal, but Strong Expressiveness breaks the tie on Criterion 2
Adequate Scalability, then Strong Extensibility, then Adequate Accessibility breaks tie with PowerSimulations
Adequate Scalability and Strong Extensibility, but Weak Accessibility drops it below PowerModels
Adequate Scalability but Adequate Extensibility (not Strong) separates it from PowerModels/PowerSimulations
Weak Scalability immediately separates it from the Adequate group

scenarios.name: Extensibility First
scenarios.description: Extensibility promoted to the highest-priority criterion, followed by Expressiveness, Scalability, Accessibility, Maturity

rankings.notes: Strong Extensibility (tied with PowerModels/PowerSimulations), then Strong Expressiveness breaks the tie on Criterion 2
Strong Extensibility, Adequate Expressiveness; Adequate Accessibility breaks tie with PowerSimulations on Criterion 4
Strong Extensibility, Adequate Expressiveness; Weak Accessibility drops it below PowerModels
Adequate Extensibility separates it from the Strong group; Adequate Expressiveness beats pandapower
Adequate Extensibility but Weak Expressiveness drops it to last

scenarios.name: Maturity and Accessibility Swapped
scenarios.description: Maturity promoted above Accessibility: Expressiveness, Extensibility, Scalability, Maturity, Accessibility

rankings.notes: Tied with PowerSimulations through Criterion 3; both Adequate Maturity; PowerModels' Adequate Accessibility breaks tie on Criterion 5
Tied with PowerModels through Criterion 4; Weak Accessibility drops it to third
Adequate Extensibility (not Strong) separates it from PowerModels/PowerSimulations
Weak Expressiveness immediately separates it on Criterion 1

summary: The ranking is completely stable across all three alternative scenarios. PyPSA holds the top position in every case because it is the only tool with Strong Expressiveness, which dominates regardless of criterion priority ordering. The full ordering also remains unchanged because tier gaps between adjacent-ranked tools are wide enough that no single priority reordering changes relative positions.
